title: I²C Real-Time Clock — DS3231 Timekeeping and Alarms
content: The DS3231 is a temperature-compensated crystal oscillator (TCXO) RTC IC with I²C interface. Accuracy: ±2 ppm (±63 s/year). Provides seconds/minutes/hours/day/date/month/year registers, two programmable alarms, and a 32 kHz output. Battery-backed VBAT input (CR2032) maintains time without main power.
extended_content: DS3231 uses an integrated 32.768 kHz crystal with automatic temperature compensation, giving much better long-term accuracy than cheaper DS1307 (no compensation). Alarms 1 and 2 assert INT/SQW pin LOW, waking a sleeping MCU via external interrupt. I²C address: 0x68 (fixed). Register map: seconds at 0x00, alarm 1 at 0x07, alarm 2 at 0x0B, control at 0x0E, status at 0x0F, temperature at 0x11 (0.25 °C resolution). Common modules include a 4 kbit AT24C32 EEPROM on the same I²C bus at 0x57. Arduino: RTClib by Adafruit simplifies date/time access. Applications: scheduled feeding, timed irrigation, data logging with UTC timestamps.
source: Maxim DS3231 Extremely Accurate I²C-Integrated RTC/TCXO/Crystal Datasheet, Rev 10
